arabic: تَقُولُ الأُسْطُورَةُ إِنَّ رَجُلًا حَكِيمًا فِي الزَّمَانِ الْقَدِيمِ اخْتَرَعَ الْحُرُوفَ الْعَرَبِيَّةَ لِيَحْفَظَ كَلَامَ النَّاسِ. رَسَمَ كُلَّ حَرْفٍ بِشَكْلٍ يُشْبِهُ مَا يَدُلُّ عَلَيْهِ فِي الطَّبِيعَةِ. أَعْطَى الْحُرُوفَ لِلنَّاسِ وَقَالَ لَهُمْ: «بِهَذِهِ الرُّمُوزِ يَبْقَى كَلَامُكُمْ بَعْدَكُمْ.» فَرِحَ النَّاسُ وَبَدَأُوا يَكْتُبُونَ أَشْعَارَهُمْ وَقِصَصَهُمْ وَأَحْلَامَهُمْ. هَكَذَا وُلِدَتِ الْكِتَابَةُ وَصَارَتِ الذَّاكِرَةُ الْكُبْرَى لِلْبَشَرِيَّةِ.
translation: Legenda kaže da je mudrac u davna vremena izumio arapska slova kako bi sačuvao govor ljudi. Svako je slovo nacrtao u obliku koji podsjeća na ono što označava u prirodi. Dao je slova narodu i rekao im: „Ovim znakovima vaš govor ostaje i nakon vas.
translationEn: Legend has it that a wise man in ancient times invented the Arabic letters to preserve the speech of the people. He drew each letter in a shape that resembled what it represented in nature. He gave the letters to the people and told them: "With these signs your speech will outlive you." The people rejoiced and began writing their poems, stories, and dreams. Thus writing was born and became humanity's greatest memory.
